description:
# Generalize inline_tail_calls_once to handle pure-tail-caller cont fns

## The smell

is_inlinable requires is_leaf (no terminating Call/TailCall). So a cont
fn whose body is just `compute pure stmts; TailCall(target, args')` —
the EXACT shape lower_multi_clause/case/with mint for recursive clause
bodies — can never be merged back into its caller. This forces
lower_multi_clause to keep an inline-body fast path (the fork +
body_might_cps_split walker, ~110 LOC).

The limitation is in the inliner, not in pattern lowering. Fix the
inliner; the fork in lowering becomes unnecessary.

## What changes

In src/ir_inline.rs:

Add `is_pure_tail_caller(f)`: single block, all stmts pure (whitelist:
Const/BinOp/UnOp/TupleField/ListHead/ListTail/ListIsNil/TypeTest/
MakeTuple/MakeList), terminator is TailCall (or TailCallClosure).

Extend `inline_tail_calls_once`: when caller has `TailCall(K, args)`
and K is_pure_tail_caller (and not closure-target, not self-recursive,
within budget): substitute args into K's entry params, splice K's
stmts before caller's terminator, replace caller's terminator with
alpha-renamed K's TailCall.

## What stays

- All existing inliner predicates (is_leaf, is_inlinable)
- Existing leaf-case path
- closure_targets exclusion
- self-recursion guard

## What's removed

Nothing from the inliner. body_might_cps_split + the lowering fork
get removed in D.1 (which depends on this ticket).

## Acceptance

- All fixtures pass (three-path parity)
- CLIF tightens OR is byte-identical for any fixture that already has
  always-cont-fn callsites (case-using, with-using, cond-using)
- New unit test: pure-tail-caller cont fn gets merged
- Budget respected (no callee with >INLINE_BUDGET stmts merges)
title: fz-ul4.43.D.0: inliner crosses pure tail-calls
